estate_add_on_options.name: Expedited Filing (Standard)
estate_add_on_options.description: Moves your estate planning documents to the front of our standard processing queue with a 5-business-day turnaround.

estate_add_on_options.name: Attorney Review & Recommendations
estate_add_on_options.description: A licensed estate planning attorney reviews your documents, suggests revisions, and answers up to 3 follow-up questions by email.

estate_add_on_options.name: Mobile Notary Service
estate_add_on_options.description: A mobile notary meets you at your home or office to notarize your estate planning documents (within 25 miles of a firm office).

estate_add_on_options.name: Annual Estate Plan Checkup
estate_add_on_options.description: One annual review of your estate planning documents for up to 3 years, with recommended updates based on law changes.

estate_add_on_options.name: Printed Binder & Digital Vault
estate_add_on_options.description: Professionally printed copies of your documents in an organized binder plus secure digital storage for 5 years.

estate_document_templates.name: Last Will and Testament
estate_document_templates.description: Directs how your assets are distributed, names an executor, and can nominate guardians for minor children.

estate_document_templates.name: Durable Power of Attorney
estate_document_templates.description: Authorizes a trusted person to handle your financial and legal affairs if you become incapacitated.

estate_document_templates.name: Advance Healthcare Directive
estate_document_templates.description: Sets out your medical treatment preferences and appoints a healthcare agent to make decisions if you cannot.

estate_document_templates.name: Revocable Living Trust
estate_document_templates.description: Helps avoid probate and manage how and when your assets are distributed during life and after death.

estate_document_templates.name: Guardianship Nomination
estate_document_templates.description: Formally nominates guardians for minor children in the event of your incapacity or death.

articles.title: California Non-Compete Agreements After Recent Reforms: What Employees Should Know
articles.summary: Learn how California’s evolving approach to non-compete and non-solicitation clauses affects employees, and what to look for before signing.
articles.content_html:
California Non-Compete Agreements After Recent Reforms

California has long taken a strict approach to non-compete agreements, but recent reforms have expanded notice requirements and increased penalties for employers who attempt to enforce void restrictions. In this article, we explain when a non-compete or non-solicitation clause may still impact you, how to spot overbroad language, and when to speak with an employment lawyer.

Key Takeaways

  • Most post-employment non-compete agreements are void in California.
  • Non-solicitation and confidentiality provisions may still be enforceable if narrowly tailored.
  • Employees should review offer letters, equity agreements, and severance packages for non-compete language.

articles.title: Non-Compete Agreement Basics for Employees
articles.summary: An overview of how non-compete agreements work, when they can be enforced, and what to consider before you sign.
articles.content_html:
Non-Compete Agreement Basics for Employees

Non-compete agreements attempt to limit where and for whom you can work after leaving your employer. Their enforceability depends heavily on state law, the scope of restrictions, and the employer’s legitimate business interests.

articles.title: How to Negotiate a Severance Package in Tech
articles.summary: Layoffs in the tech industry often come with severance offers. Here’s what to look for and how an employment lawyer can help.
articles.content_html:
How to Negotiate a Severance Package in Tech

When you receive a severance offer, you may have more room to negotiate than you think. We cover common terms, release language, non-disparagement clauses, and how to evaluate whether the payment fairly reflects your situation.

articles.title: How Much Does an Uncontested Divorce Cost in California?
articles.summary: Understand the typical court fees, attorney fees, and fixed-fee options for a standard uncontested divorce in California.
articles.content_html:
How Much Does an Uncontested Divorce Cost in California?

For many couples, an uncontested divorce is the most efficient and affordable path to dissolve a marriage. In California, total costs typically include court filing fees, service of process, and attorney fees. Our online fee estimator helps you compare scenarios based on whether you have minor children or contested property.

articles.title: Parenting Plans 101: Creating a Custody Schedule That Works
articles.summary: A practical guide to building a parenting plan that focuses on your child’s needs while reducing future conflict.
articles.content_html:
Parenting Plans 101

A clear parenting plan can prevent many of the misunderstandings that fuel post-divorce conflict. We outline common custody schedules, decision-making authority, holiday rotations, and communication guidelines.

articles.title: Estate Planning Checklist for Young Families
articles.summary: Four core documents every young family should consider, plus common mistakes to avoid.
articles.content_html:
Estate Planning Checklist for Young Families

If you have children or are planning to start a family, a basic estate plan can provide essential protections. We cover last wills, guardianship nominations, powers of attorney, and advance healthcare directives.

articles.title: Last Will vs. Living Trust: Which Do You Need?
articles.summary: Compare the benefits and tradeoffs of a last will and a revocable living trust so you can choose the right foundation for your estate plan.
articles.content_html:
Last Will vs. Living Trust

Both wills and revocable living trusts can direct how your property is distributed, but they differ in cost, complexity, privacy, and how they handle incapacity. We break down when each tool makes sense and when a combination may be best.

articles.title: Choosing the Right Business Entity in Illinois
articles.summary: LLC, corporation, or sole proprietorship? Understand your options when forming a business in Illinois.
articles.content_html:
Choosing the Right Business Entity in Illinois

The entity you choose affects your personal liability, taxes, and ability to raise capital. This article compares LLCs, corporations, and other structures under Illinois law.

articles.title: What to Do if Your Landlord Won't Return Your Security Deposit in New York
articles.summary: Steps New York tenants can take when a landlord withholds a security deposit without explanation.
articles.content_html:
New York Security Deposit Rules for Tenants

New York law limits how much a landlord can charge for a security deposit and requires itemized statements when deductions are taken. If your landlord refuses to return your deposit, you may be able to send a formal demand letter or file a claim in small claims court.

articles.title: How to Prepare for Your First Meeting With a Lawyer
articles.summary: A simple checklist to help you make the most of your initial consultation.
articles.content_html:
How to Prepare for Your First Meeting With a Lawyer

Bringing the right documents and questions to your first meeting can save time and reduce stress. We outline what to gather and what to expect in your consultation.

articles.title: Remote Work Policies and Wage-and-Hour Compliance
articles.summary: Key wage-and-hour issues employers should address in remote and hybrid work policies.
articles.content_html:
Remote Work Policies and Wage-and-Hour Compliance

As more teams work remotely, employers must update policies to address timekeeping, overtime, expense reimbursement, and off-the-clock work risks.

lawyers.bio: Laura Nguyen focuses her practice on business formation, commercial contracts, and outside general counsel services for small and mid-sized companies in the Chicago area.
Michael represents closely held businesses and startups in contract negotiations, vendor agreements, and day-to-day corporate matters.
David advises employers on employment policies and represents companies in complex commercial and employment disputes.
Sophia represents employees in severance negotiations, non-compete disputes, and discrimination matters, with a focus on the tech industry.
Daniel counsels employees and executives nationwide on wrongful termination, wage-and-hour claims, and whistleblower retaliation.
Emily focuses on divorce, custody, and support matters, with an emphasis on low-conflict, settlement-oriented approaches.
Karen represents Bay Area parents in custody, visitation, and child support matters, including move-away and complex parenting plans.
Rashida designs comprehensive estate plans for professionals and business owners, including wills, trusts, and incapacity planning.
James advocates for residential and small commercial tenants in disputes over security deposits, repairs, and lease enforcement.
Olivia helps Texas families create practical estate plans, including wills, powers of attorney, and advance healthcare directives.
Henry advises startups and mid-sized companies on employment policies, handbooks, and wage-and-hour compliance, particularly for remote teams.
Marco provides outside general counsel services to growth-stage companies, with a focus on SaaS and professional services firms.

services.name: Uncontested Divorce
services.short_description: Flat-fee representation for standard uncontested divorces with no minor children and no contested property.
services.long_description: Our uncontested divorce package is designed for spouses who agree on all major issues and want to move through the process efficiently. The package includes document preparation, filing, and guidance through final judgment. Use our online fee estimator to see costs based on your county and case details.

services.name: Contested Divorce Litigation
services.short_description: Hourly representation in contested divorce cases involving disputes over custody, support, or property.
services.long_description: For higher-conflict cases, we provide strategic representation in court and in negotiations, with a focus on long-term financial and parenting outcomes.

services.name: Child Custody & Support
services.short_description: Representation in initial custody and support matters and post-judgment modifications.
services.long_description: We help parents develop practical parenting plans, calculate child support, and seek modifications when circumstances change.

services.name: Non-Compete Agreement Review
services.short_description: Attorney review of your non-compete, non-solicitation, and related restrictive covenants.
services.long_description: An experienced employment lawyer will review your agreement, assess enforceability under applicable state law, and provide practical advice on negotiation strategies and risk.

services.name: Wrongful Termination Consultation
services.short_description: Initial case evaluation for employees who believe they were wrongfully terminated.
services.long_description: We review your termination circumstances, contracts, and key documents to assess whether you may have legal claims and discuss potential fee structures, including contingency where appropriate.

services.name: Severance Agreement Review
services.short_description: Flat-fee review of your severance package and release of claims.
services.long_description: An attorney will explain key terms, identify problematic clauses (such as non-compete or non-disparagement provisions), and suggest revisions you can request from your employer.

services.name: Custom Estate Plan Package
services.short_description: Build a customized estate planning package with wills, powers of attorney, and healthcare directives.
services.long_description: Use our online builder to choose the documents you need, including a Last Will, Durable Power of Attorney, and Advance Healthcare Directive. Adjust options and add-ons in real time to keep your package within your budget before scheduling a review with an estate planning attorney.

services.name: Standalone Last Will
services.short_description: Preparation of a standalone Last Will and Testament tailored to your state.
services.long_description: This service is ideal if you want a simple will without a full trust-based plan. It includes attorney consultation, drafting, and one round of revisions.

services.name: Revocable Living Trust Package
services.short_description: Comprehensive trust-based estate plan, including pour-over will and powers of attorney.
services.long_description: Designed for clients who want to avoid probate and provide detailed instructions for incapacity and death, this package includes a revocable living trust, pour-over will, financial power of attorney, and healthcare documents.

services.name: Outside General Counsel
services.short_description: Ongoing legal support for growing businesses without in-house counsel.
services.long_description: We provide flexible monthly plans for contract review, policy drafting, and general advisory work so your leadership team has ready access to legal guidance.

services.name: Business Contract Review
services.short_description: Flat-fee review and redline of standard commercial contracts.
services.long_description: Ideal for vendor agreements, NDAs, and customer contracts up to a specified page limit. Includes a follow-up call to discuss changes.

services.name: LLC & Corporation Formation
services.short_description: Form your LLC or corporation and receive customized governing documents.
services.long_description: We help you choose the right entity type, file formation documents, obtain tax IDs, and draft operating agreements or bylaws tailored to your ownership structure.

services.name: Tenant Security Deposit Dispute
services.short_description: Flat-fee assistance for tenants whose security deposits are being wrongfully withheld.
services.long_description: We review your lease, move-in and move-out documentation, and communications with your landlord, then help you pursue return of your deposit through demand letters or small claims court.

services.name: Landlord Eviction Services
services.short_description: Representation for landlords in residential eviction proceedings.
services.long_description: We assist landlords with notices, filings, court appearances, and settlement negotiations in compliance with local rent control and just-cause ordinances.

services.name: Residential Lease Review
services.short_description: Attorney review of your residential lease before you sign.
services.long_description: We identify unfair or risky provisions in your lease, explain your rights and obligations, and suggest revisions you can request from your landlord.

legal_tools.name: Non-Disclosure Agreement (NDA) Generator
legal_tools.description: Create custom unilateral or mutual NDAs online, including governing law, term length, and optional non-solicitation clauses, then generate a downloadable preview to review with a lawyer if needed.

legal_tools.name: Legal Fee & Budget Estimator
legal_tools.description: Estimate your total legal fees for select flat-fee and hourly services, including uncontested divorce and security deposit disputes, based on your location and case complexity.

legal_tools.name: Estate Planning Package Builder
legal_tools.description: Interactively build an estate planning package by selecting documents such as a Last Will, Durable Power of Attorney, and Advance Healthcare Directive, applying add-ons while keeping your total within budget.
